Box.Box: Misfits: Nuclear Wasteland – Server Rules

Misfits: Nuclear Wasteland is a Medium Roleplay Fallout server. The goal is not only engaging gameplay, but the creation of memorable stories shaped by player choice.
The Wasteland is harsh and consequences matter. Your survival depends on your actions, your alliances, and your judgment. Administrative intervention is rare and reserved for rule enforcement, not story rescue.
Rule 0: Conduct

This is a collaborative environment. Toxicity, ego play, and behavior designed to ruin others’ enjoyment are not tolerated.
Play to create story, not to “win” at any cost.
Respect administrators and developers. They are volunteers.
Cooperate during admin interactions. Do not disconnect during an active ticket without valid reason.
Report technical issues promptly.
Administration may intervene in situations not explicitly covered by the rules if behavior damages the community or the round.
Punishments are issued at staff discretion based on severity, context, and history.
Rule 1: Griefing

Griefing is any action taken primarily to disrupt gameplay or degrade the experience of others without meaningful in-character reasoning.
This includes:
Deliberate destruction with no RP justification.
Trap placement intended only to frustrate.
Causing large-scale disruption without narrative basis.
Lack of intent does not always excuse large-scale damage. If your actions severely impact the round without reasonable RP logic, they may be treated as griefing.
Rule 2: Fair Play

2.1 Metagaming
Using out-of-character knowledge to influence in-character actions is prohibited.
Information from Discord, streams, OOC chat, or ghosting may not be used IC.
Coordinated external communication affecting the round is forbidden.
Streaming is allowed, but responsibility for information leaks lies with the streamer.
Metagaming violations may result in removal.

2.2 Multiaccounting
Using multiple accounts in the same round is forbidden.
Ban evasion is strictly prohibited.
Creating alternate accounts to avoid punishment history is not allowed.
Violations may result in permanent bans.

2.3 Exploits
No cheats, scripts, macros, or third-party tools.
No intentional abuse of bugs.
No deliberate server strain.
Report discovered bugs through official channels.
Rule 3: Roleplay Standards

Characters must fit the Fallout setting in tone, behavior, and naming.
3.1 Character Identity
No meme names or obvious real-world references.
No reuse of deceased or permanently imprisoned characters without approval.
Administration may require name changes for immersion reasons.
3.2 Communication
Do not break immersion with mechanical terms or real-world memes.
Keep OOC topics out of IC channels.
OOC channels must remain respectful and free of political or extremist discussion.
3.3 Atmosphere
Avoid referencing game systems or “admins” in character.
Provide in-character explanations for absence when possible.
Do not create offensive or explicit material using in-game media.
3.4 Knowledge Limits
Your character is not all-knowing.
Do not reference unresearched advanced technology.
Stay within the expertise of your chosen role.
Rule 4: Erotic Roleplay

Explicit ERP is prohibited in all forms, including written in-game material.
Light romantic interaction such as flirting, hugging, or kissing is allowed if non-explicit and consensual.
If instructed by staff to stop behavior near the boundary, you must comply immediately.
Violations may result in permanent removal.
Rule 5: SSD Protection

Characters who disconnect (SSD) may not be directly harmed.
They may be looted, but not killed or kidnapped.
Disconnecting during active combat or escalation removes this protection.
SSD characters inside faction bases during active raids are not protected.
Rule 6: Armed Conflict

6.1 Escalation
Dialogue must precede lethal force in most situations.
Escalation must be face-to-face.
A clear demand must be given.
A reasonable opportunity to respond must exist.
Exceptions include active firefights, immediate threats, wildlife, faction wars, and designated high-risk zones.

6.2 Robbery
Do not strip victims of all survival means.
Non-resisting victims must be left alive.
Resistance shifts responsibility for outcome.

6.3 Faction Warfare
Factions begin neutral.
Only recognized leaders may formally declare war.
War begins five minutes after a face-to-face declaration.
Killing diplomats or surrendered individuals is forbidden.

6.4 Early Raids
Faction bases may not be raided within the first 30 minutes of the round.
Rule 7: New Life Rule

7.1 Within a Round
You may not rejoin on the same character to bypass death or punishment.
Revived characters forget the circumstances of their death and the final moments preceding it.
7.2 Between Rounds
Do not use knowledge from previous rounds for advantage.
Characters do not remember contradictory past events.
Administrative Authority

Project leadership and senior moderation retain final authority in rule interpretation.
The server maintains a 16+ expectation.
Rules may evolve as the project develops.
